title: Trisetum parviflorum
taxonomy: Trisetum parviflorum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Trisetum, which includes several species of grasses. The genus Trisetum is classified within the subfamily Pooideae, which is one of the two subfamilies of the family Poaceae. The subfamily Pooideae is a large and diverse group of grasses that also includes the genera Festuca and Lolium. Trisetum parviflorum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia.

intro: Trisetum parviflorum, also known as Small-flowered hairgrass, is a perennial grass species native to the temperate regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Trisetum parviflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
